claim: The zodiac and astronomical sections use a label-encoding scheme where words are proper nouns or fixed labels (star names, month names, zodiac terms) rather than running prose, which explains their anomalously low entropy (3.7149 and 3.7471 bits) as a reduced effective vocabulary with high repetition of a small label lexicon.
evidence_for: Zodiac entropy 3.7149 bits is the lowest of all sections â€” consistent with a small fixed vocabulary of labels
Astronomical entropy 3.7471 bits is second-lowest, consistent with the same label-encoding pattern
Medieval astronomical manuscripts routinely use repeated short labels for stars, months, and zodiac signs
These sections are visually dominated by figures with attached short word sequences, matching a label structure
evidence_against: If labels, the type-token ratio and average word length should be distinctly lower than in herbal/recipes sections â€” this needs verification
Some zodiac folios contain longer text passages that may not be pure labels
test: Compute average word length, type-token ratio (TTR), and hapax ratio separately for zodiac and astronomical sections vs. herbal and recipes sections. Additionally, measure the frequency of the top-10 words within each section as a fraction of total section word count.
test_metric: TTR per section; mean word length per section; top-10-word coverage fraction per section
test_threshold: If zodiac/astronomical TTR < 0.20 AND top-10-word coverage > 25% of section tokens, label-encoding hypothesis is supported. If TTR > 0.35 or top-10 coverage < 10%, the label hypothesis is weakened.

tests_run.test: compute average word length, type-token ratio (ttr), and hapax ratio separately for zodiac and astro
tests_run.details: Observed hapax ratio: 0.701. Expected: ~0.50. Voynich has unusually high hapax ratio (70.1%), suggesting either large vocabulary, verbose encoding, or noise.

reasoning: Label-encoding is the most parsimonious explanation for the entropy gradient across sections. Confirming it would allow the low-entropy sections to be analyzed separately as a constrained vocabulary problem, significantly simplifying the decipherment task for those sections.
